svg.text: AnimaMesh — the engine and the instance
Two repos, a one-way dependency. The public engine is generic logic; a private brain is one company's knowledge, config, and secrets.
anima-mesh — the ENGINE (public, on GitHub)
Generic logic that animates any brain. Never names a
company, person, or persona — identity is configuration.
src/ — the logic
harness/ — heartbeat + direction cores (the run loop)
providers/ — the model chokepoint (moonshot, anthropic, …)
gates/ + autonomy/ — the L1–L4 ladder, enforced in code
ledger/ + okf/ — append-only audit + the bundle format
instance/ — the storage seam: store-fs · store-github
channels/ + a2a/ — Discord/Gmail/Notion + agent card
init/ + cli.ts — scaffold a brain · run · gate · report
Safety lives here, in deterministic code — gates, ladder,
ledger, verifiers. Everything else is model judgment.
workers/ — the cloud tier (deployed per instance)
heartbeat/ — daily beat + inbound directions (2 DOs)
web/ — the Google-OIDC dashboard
templates/ + docs/
templates/agents/ — the roster, with {{PLACEHOLDER}} identity
docs/ — this shelf (architecture, runbooks, learnings)
The one hard rule
No company name, real email, secret, or persona ever
appears in engine code, templates, tests, or docs.
A capability built in a brain for expedience is flagged
"generalize me", then promoted here DE-IDENTIFIED —
placeholders for identity, config for choices.
Upgraded deliberately: released as a git tag vX.Y.Z.
your brain — the INSTANCE (private, yours)
One company's whole back office as plain files. All real
data. Pins the engine by tag; stays private forever.
animamesh.config.json — the pairing
engine.ref (pin) · cognition.overrides · direction/delivery
· identity/persona · D11 activation gates
bundle/ — the knowledge (one concept per file)
constitution.md — immutable hard limits (machine-read)
facts/ — stable facts (status: verified / unverified)
decisions/ — dated, immutable; supersede, never edit
events/ — append-only; corrections are new events
agents/*.md — each agent IS a concept (level·model·harness)
ops/calendar.md — what agents wake up to check
ops/nags.md — reminders that repeat until done
index.md · log.md — reserved (log is append-only)
readable by any human in any editor; hostage to no vendor
state + run artifacts (git IS the database)
ledger/actions.jsonl — append-only action ledger (audit)
approvals/ — the file-based "needs you" gate
reports/ · drafts/ — briefs, direction replies, L2 drafts
cloud/ — this company's wrangler deploy configs
.env.local — secrets (git-ignored, mode 600)
API keys, OAuth tokens, bot tokens — referenced by NAME
in config and code, never committed, never echoed.
Cloud secrets live on the Worker (wrangler secret put).
The engine can read this brain over a local path OR over
GitHub — same files, either runtime (the storage seam).
depends on
pins engine @vX.Y.Z
"generalize me"
promote, de-identified
one engine,
many companies
brain-acme
brain-beta
The engine is a codebase you pin, not a service you share. Nothing about your company lives in it, so it upgrades independently and any AI model can read it.
The brain is everything about your company as git-tracked markdown + JSON. To start company #2, you make a second brain and point it at the same engine tag.
Both runtimes write here. The laptop CLI and the cloud Worker both run the engine over this brain and commit their work — so a local clone must git pull to stay in sync.
